title: Anti-tumor efficacy of a potent and selective non-covalent KRASG12D inhibitor
experiment_summary: Evaluation of MRTX1133, a non-covalent small molecule inhibitor targeting KRASG12D mutant protein, tested for binding affinity, selectivity, and anti-tumor efficacy in cell lines and xenograft models
hypothesis: A high-affinity, non-covalent small molecule can selectively inhibit KRASG12D mutant protein and demonstrate anti-tumor activity in KRASG12D-driven cancers
methodology: Biochemical binding assays, cell viability assays, western blotting, RNA-seq, and mouse xenograft tumor studies to evaluate MRTX1133 efficacy

columns.name: pdb_file
columns.description: PDB file path or content for the target protein structure (KRASG12D or KRASWT)
constraints: Must be valid PDB file path

columns.name: ligand_smiles
columns.description: SMILES string representation of the small molecule ligand being tested

columns.name: sequence
columns.description: Amino acid sequence of the KRAS protein variant
constraints: Must be valid amino acid sequence

columns.name: cell_line
columns.description: Cancer cell line used in the experiment
constraints: Must be established cancer cell line

columns.name: drug_concentration
columns.description: Concentration of MRTX1133 used in the assay

columns.name: ic50_binding
columns.description: Half maximal inhibitory concentration in binding assays

columns.name: cell_viability_percent
columns.description: Percentage of viable cells after treatment relative to control

relationships: KRASG12D mutations show higher binding affinity (lower KD) to MRTX1133 compared to KRASWT
Drug concentration inversely correlates with cell viability in KRASG12D cell lines
pERK inhibition correlates with reduced cell viability and tumor regression
Treatment duration affects the degree of pathway inhibition and cell death

group_definitions.treatment_group: Cells or animals treated with MRTX1133 at various concentrations
group_definitions.control_group: Cells or animals treated with vehicle control (DMSO)
group_definitions.mutation_negative: Cell lines with wild-type KRAS or other mutations

notes: MRTX1133 shows ~700-fold selectivity for KRASG12D over KRASWT
The drug binds to both GDP-bound and GTP-bound states of KRASG12D
Pancreatic cancer models show higher response rates (73%) than colorectal cancer models (25%)
The all-atom-protein-design model can evaluate binding poses and affinities for this protein-ligand system

selected_model.reason: This model is specifically designed for protein-drug design tasks including structure-based drug design and evaluating small molecule binding to proteins. It can analyze the KRASG12D-MRTX1133 interaction at atomic detail, predict binding affinities, and potentially suggest modifications to improve selectivity or potency.
selected_model.name: All Atom Protein Design

pdbFile.description: Input structure containing target protein and/or ligand in PDB format

targetChains.description: Chain IDs of the target molecule

binderLength.description: Length or length range of the designed binder

hotspots.description: Target residues to focus binder design on
